senses.definition: An archaic or scholarly term related to scazon, a form of classical poetry with a halting metre.
examples: In his study, he discusses scazontics as a subset of scazon poetry.
The manuscript contains notes on scazontics and its metrical peculiarities.

senses.definition: Likely a misspelling of sclera, the white outer layer of the eyeball.
examples: The doctor examined the scclera for any signs of irritation.
A healthy scclera indicates good eye health.

senses.definition: A small, thick Anglo-Saxon coin made of silver (or occasionally gold, or copper).
examples: Coins called sceats were used in Anglo-Saxon England.
The hoard included several sceats with intricate designs.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of sceat, an Anglo-Saxon coin.
examples: The manuscript references a sceatt as part of the king's tribute.
Collectors seek out rare sceatt imitations.

senses.definition: Plural form of sceatta; multiple small Anglo-Saxon coins.
examples: The hoard contained several sceattas and other small silver coins.
Sceattas were imported and minted in early medieval trade.

senses.definition: A misspelling or variant form of schedule; a plan or timetable of activities.
examples: Please consult the scedule for the seminar times.
The travel scedule was updated last night.

senses.definition: Misspelled or archaic past participle of schedule; having been planned to occur at a certain time.
examples: The event is sceduled for Friday.
All activities are sceduled in advance.

senses.definition: Plural of schedule; lists of planned times or sequences of activities.
examples: The conference provides several different scedules to choose from.
Check the scedules to see when your sessions run.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of scheduling; the process of planning when tasks will happen.
examples: Effective sceduling improves project timelines.
They are responsible for sceduling all meetings.

senses.definition: A person who scorns or scoffs; one who displays scornful or derisive behavior.
examples: The critic acted as a scegger, dismissing the artist's work outright.

senses.definition: A medical term for pain in a limb or limb-like structures; relates to scelo- (galling) and algia (pain).
examples: The patient reported scelalgia in the affected limb.

senses.definition: Relating to or involving crime or criminals; derived from Latin root sceler- meaning evil.
examples: A scelerat act shocked the community.

senses.definition: To make evil or to cause to become criminal; to transform for evil ends.
examples: To scelerate their aims would require corrupting the system.

senses.definition: People who commit evil acts; criminals (plural).
examples: The records show a group of scelerates operating in the region.

senses.definition: A genus or clade of armored, extinct reptiles belonging to the ankylosaur group.
examples: Fossils of scelidosaur illustrate early armored dinosaurs.
Paleontologists study scelidosaur remains to understand dinosaur evolution.

senses.definition: A member of the group or a description of creatures related to scelidosaurs.
examples: Scelidosaurian remains provide insight into early ankylosaurs.

senses.definition: A taxonomic grouping related to scelidosaurs; often used for early armored dinosaurs.
examples: Researchers classified the specimen as scelidosauroid.

senses.definition: A genus of early armored dinosaurs from the Jurassic period.
examples: Scelidosaurus is a well-known early armored dinosaur.
Fossils of scelidosaurus help scientists study dinosaur armor.

senses.definition: An extinct mammal from the family of animals related to armadillos; part of the extinct larger group of glyptodonts.
examples: Scelidotherium fossils have been found in South America.

senses.definition: A genus of potter wasps; insects that build pot-like nests from mud.
examples: Sceliphron wasps are known for their distinctive mud nests.

senses.definition: A taxonomic genus name; likely a now-obsolete or obscure term in entomology or mycology.
examples: The old catalog lists a specimen under sceloncus.

senses.definition: A genus of spiny lizards common in North and Central America.
examples: Sceloporus species are often called spiny lizards.

senses.definition: A taxonomic name; possibly a plant or animal genus; very obscure.
examples: The herbarium note mentions scelotyrbe.

senses.definition: An archaic or dialectal term; may refer to a blade or cutting edge in some contexts, or be a misspelling.
examples: The old manual describes the scelp as a sharp driving tool.
